{{Quote|Aeron thinks someone has been selling us out. She thinks there must be a stinking Viraxo mole in our organization. To be sure she wants to check the communication records and logs from home. The problem is the Comm Datacore was removed along with most of the other equipment. Aeron did some slicing and found out that the datacore ended up at an Imperial scrap yard.|Emon Azzameen|<ref name="XWA" />}}
The '''Comm Datacore''' was owned and utilized by the [[Azzameen family]] to send and receive all [[Comm unit/Legends|communications]] through the family's holdings. Originally, the [[Datacore/Legends|datacore]] was installed within the family's [[Space station/Legends|space facility]], [[Azzameen Station/Legends|Azzameen Station]]. However, after the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] witnessed the Azzameen family's [[Battle of Hospital|involvement]] with the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]], the Imperials declared the Azzameens traitors and moved to [[Imperialization/Legends|nationalize]] [[Imperialization of Azzameen assets|all Azzameen property]]. Once the Empire drove the Azzameens from their [[Orbit/Legends|orbital]] home, Azzameen Station was stripped of most of its installed equipment. The Comm Datacore was sent to an [[Junkyard Control|Imperial scrapyard]] near [[Tolig IV]], where it was stored in a [[Class-K cargo container|cargo container]].

Later, [[Emon Azzameen]] and his younger [[Gender/Legends|brother]], [[Ace Azzameen/Legends|Ace]], conducted a [[Mission to recover Comm Datacore|mission]] to recover the datacore in order to verify their suspicions that someone among their employ was leaking information to the Azzameens' enemies. Amidst the resulting battle at the scrapyard between the Azzameen brothers and a group of raiders, family [[MK-series maintenance droid|droid]] [[MK-09/Legends|MK-09]] retrieved the [[Cargo container/Legends|cargo container]] holding the Comm Datacore. Emon then gave the recovered data to his sister, [[Aeron Azzameen|Aeron]], who in turn [[Slicer/Legends|sliced]] through the logs to find any relevant communications.

==Description==
{{Quote|Aeron believed she could slice into this data core and find out why Azzameen security was compromised so often.|MK-09 explains why the Azzameens needed to recover the Comm Datacore|<ref name="XWA" />}}
The Comm Datacore<ref name="XWA" /> was a [[Comm unit/Legends|communications]] [[Datacore/Legends|datacore]]<ref name="XWAPrima" /> that was originally installed in [[Azzameen Station/Legends|Azzameen Station]]. The datacore was responsible for facilitating and logging communications through the [[Space station/Legends|orbital installation]] owned and operated by the [[Azzameen family]]. Family [[Technician/Legends|technician]] [[Aeron Azzameen]] presumed that the datacore also monitored transmissions sent without the Azzameens' knowledge, such as those possibly made by rogue agents under Azzameen employ who were working against the family and its [[Merchant/Legends|trading]] company, [[Twin Suns Transport Services/Legends|Twin Suns Transport Services]]. The Comm Datacore stored records of both inbound and outbound communications, which could be preserved, accessed, and downloaded even if the core was removed from the station. The datacore was small enough to fit inside a seven-[[Meter/Legends|meter]]-long [[Class-K cargo container]].<ref name="XWA" />

==History==
{{Quote|Aeron's sources say the datacore has been dumped off at a scrap yard near Tolig IV. With luck, it will be there when you and Emon arrive.|MK-09 briefs Ace Azzameen on the mission to recover the family datacore|<ref name="XWA" />}}
[[File:Class-K_Cargo_Container.jpg|thumb|right|180px|The discarded Comm Datacore was stored in a Class-K cargo container at Junkyard Control.]]
At some point by [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]],<ref name="Dating">According to ''[[Star Wars: X-Wing Alliance]]'', the Comm Datacore was removed from [[Azzameen Station/Legends|Azzameen Station]] at some point prior to the [[Battle of the Bajic Shipyards]], which ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates to [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], thereby providing an estimated [[Time/Legends|time]] frame for the [[Datacore/Legends|datacore's]] installation.</ref> the Comm Database was installed in Azzameen Station, home of the Azzameen family,<ref name="XWA" /> located in the [[Garis sector/Legends|Garis sector]] of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]].<ref name="EAOC">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}} — Based on corresponding data for [[Azzameen Station system/Legends|Azzameen Station system]]</ref> The Azzameens used the datacore to monitor and log communications through the facility.<ref name="XWA" />

Shortly after the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Corrupter Task Force|forces]] discovered that members of the Azzameen family had been [[Battle of Hospital|aiding]] the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]]. The Empire responded by issuing arrest warrants for the Azzameens and moving to seize all Azzameen property. Following a [[Imperialization of Azzameen assets|series of skirmishes]] between the Empire and the Azzameen family, Azzameen Station fell to Imperial control, and the Azzameens were driven to seek refuge with the [[Alliance Fleet/Legends|Alliance Fleet]]. Azzameen Station was stripped of much of its internal equipment, including the Comm Datacore. The datacore was eventually delivered to [[Junkyard Control]], an Imperial scrapyard located near the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Tolig IV]], where it was stored in a Class-K [[Cargo container/Legends|cargo container]].<ref name="XWA" />

After several encounters with interlopers meddling in the Azzameens' attempts to restore their dissolved company, Aeron Azzameen began to research the Comm Datacore's location,<ref name="XWA" /> as she figured that the datacore could be [[Slicer/Legends|sliced]] to reveal a rogue agent within the Azzameens who was leaking information regarding the family's affairs. Her sources soon led her to Junkyard Control.<ref name="XWAPrima" /> With the apparent location of the Comm Datacore found, Aeron called upon her [[Gender/Legends|brothers]], [[Emon Azzameen|Emon]] and [[Rebel pilot/Legends|Rebellion pilot]] [[Ace Azzameen/Legends|Ace]], as well as family [[MK-series maintenance droid|droid]] [[MK-09/Legends|MK-09]], to investigate the scrapyard and [[Mission to recover Comm Datacore|retrieve]] the logs stored within the datacore. After finding their property among the scrapyard's containers and a brief skirmish with a raiding party seeking to disrupt the Azzameen effort, MK-09 downloaded the datacore's information, which remained undamaged throughout the battle. Emon then collected the data and delivered it to Aeron.<ref name="XWA" />

==Behind the scenes==
The Comm Datacore appeared in the [[1999]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: X-Wing Alliance]]''. Later missions reveal that [[Antan Azzameen]]—co-owner of Twin Suns Transport Services and uncle to Aeron, Emon, and Ace—was the mole within the family, acting with the Imperials in order to restore the family business. However, neither the game nor its accompanying [[Prima Games]] [[Star Wars: X-Wing Alliance: Prima's Official Strategy Guide|strategy guide]] elaborate upon the Comm Datacore's role in this discovery. In the game's mission to recover the datacore, the destruction of the K-class container—and, thus, the datacore inside—causes an immediate failure, as does the destruction of MK-09 before, during, and after the information transfer.

==Appearances==
*''[[Star Wars: X-Wing Alliance]]'' {{1st}}
